rows.notes: Page title / section heading centered at top. Despite bleed-through across the top of the page the heading is legible and contains no amounts. Vertical ruling lines faint but not needed for this header.

Pounds '17' and shillings '10' read clearly in the pounds/shillings columns after projecting column boundaries from lower clear totals. Pence position shows a dash/overlined zero (per page guidance) — interpreted as 0 pence. Moderate bleed-through but digits align well with column rules.

Handwriting and bleed make shillings/pence ambiguous. Pounds column shows a small '1' fairly clearly. The shillings glyph reads like '7' but could possibly be '1' or '0'; pence appears as a small loop resembling '6'. Chosen reading '1 7s 6d' based on alignment with ruling lines; confidence lowered for ambiguity.

Pounds column shows a clear '1'. The shillings column is faint and appears as '0' (or blank); pence looks like '7'. Bleed-through complicates the pence stroke; chosen '1 0s 7d' as best fit with column alignment.

rows.description: paid for flooring of Room over ye new Bursary
rows.notes: Pounds read as '7' and shillings clearly as '10'. Pence shows a dash/overline (page guidance: read as 0). Bleed-through present but column alignment consistent with lower totals.

rows.description: paid Dav. Collin in full of his first Bill of Candles
rows.notes: Large, clear '21' in pounds column and '10' shillings visible; pence is a dash/0. This line is one of the clearest monetary lines on the page and was used to help verify column alignment.

rows.description: paid Dod for lighting of Winter Lamps
rows.notes: Pounds '1' readable. Shillings look like '3' though the top of the numeral is slightly faded by bleed-through. Pence shows dash/0. Chosen '1 3s 0d' based on alignment and stroke shapes.

rows.description: paid Cook and Dinner & xmas Gaudies
rows.notes: Shillings read clearly as '15' and pence as '4'. Pounds appear as a small '1'. Although there is some bleed-through, the '15 4d' is distinct and within valid ranges.

rows.description: paid into ye Treasury Chest 200 Guineas
rows.notes: This is the payment in for '200 Guineas' which conventionally equals 210 pounds; the pounds column clearly shows '210' and shillings '4' is visible. Pence is a dash/0. This row is one of the clearest anchors for column alignment.
Pounds column blank/zero; shillings appear as '1'. Pence column shows a dash/0. Small amount and some bleed makes the pounds column ambiguous (interpreted as 0).

rows.description: paid mr Horner for Battle Books, Ink & Paper
rows.notes: Small amount; pounds column blank. Shillings read as '1' and pence faintly as '1' (could be 0 or 1). Chosen '0 1s 1d' as best reading from faint strokes and alignment with columns.

rows.description: paid the Cooks Bill for washing of Hall, mops &c
rows.notes: Pounds '3', shillings '13' and pence '4' are relatively clear and consistent with the style of numerals used elsewhere on the page. Bleed-through minimal here compared with upper rows.
Subtotal labeled 'Soluta' with '278 7s 4d' clearly written in the right-hand columns. This line was used as part of the column alignment reference (rows 15-18 used for final refinement).
'Ditto' subtotal line with '73 19s 2d'. Amounts are legible though there is slight bleed; validated to be within currency ranges.

Primary page alignment reference per page guidance. 'Summa totalis solutorum' and the total '874 17s 10d' are very clear and used to fix the pounds/shillings/pence vertical boundaries for the rest of the page.

rows.description: Ex Receptis quae sunt ab Anni precedentis
rows.notes: Receipts brought forward from previous year. Pounds and shillings read as '1043 1s 10d' — digits are reasonably clear in lower part of page where bleed is minimal. Treated as an entry/receipt rather than a computed sum.
'Deductis Solutis' is a computed deduction and repeats the '874 17s 10d' figure from the 'Summa' line; clearly written and aligned. Classified as a total (deduction).

Balance carried forward / remainder. The figure '168 4s 9d' is readable in the lower-right group; small discrepancy with simple subtraction (see notes) is typical of carried computations in historic ledgers but the written digits themselves are clear. Classified as a total (computed remainder).
